observation: Actor attempted roughly 30 SSH authentications against a single host inside 90 seconds across a small account list; Wazuh rule 5712 fired on the burst and the source IP was tagged before any single credential pair was relevant. Defender did not need to evaluate password quality — the volume detector resolved the case from rate alone.

Actor staged the payload as a base64-encoded second argument to `sh -c` inside the container, expecting the container's audit pipeline to surface only `/bin/sh` without the inline script body. The defender pulled the host-side `docker exec` syscall record and recovered the full base64 blob from the host-side argv fork; the container-side audit absence was irrelevant because the host record carried the args.

Actor ran ssh-keyscan against the bastion host to fingerprint the SSH banner before staging the spray. Wazuh rule 5701 (SSH protocol probe / banner fetch) fired on the bare TCP connection and was associated with the eventual spray source IP via the asset graph, surfacing the pre-stage recon to the defender during triage.

Actor staggered the spray correctly at 1 attempt per 30 seconds, so rule 5712 stayed silent, but every username/password pair came from a 2019 public breach corpus that the defender's auth pipeline already enriches against. The match-on-known-breach enricher flagged each attempt independently of volume, so the spray surfaced credential-by-credential despite the careful pacing.
